stories.title: Garfield Township freezes data centers after sloppy project filing
stories.dek: Garfield Township supervisors moved to block new data centers for at least a year after an incomplete application arrived. Residents near the proposed site now face uncertainty over power lines, water use and property values. The pause gives the township time to write rules before any large project locks in.
stories.source_title: Garfield Township Tees Up Data Center Moratorium; Incomplete Application Submitted for Project - The Ticker

stories.title: DTE customers may foot bigger bills as data centers boost utility profits
stories.dek: DTE Energy’s Q2 preview highlights data-center growth as a key earnings driver for the Detroit-based utility. That expansion means more demand on the grid, which often translates into higher rates or new infrastructure costs passed to households. Michigan ratepayers have already seen added charges tied to similar projects elsewhere in the service territory.
stories.source_title: DTE Energy: Q2 Earnings Preview, A Regulated Utility With Data-Center Tailwinds (NYSE:DTE) - Seeking Alpha

stories.title: Calhoun County weighs year-long halt on new data centers over jobs and power
stories.dek: Commissioners are debating a moratorium after hearing concerns about electricity strain and whether promised economic gains will reach local taxpayers. A pause would delay projects that could draw hundreds of megawatts and millions of gallons of water. Residents in Battle Creek and surrounding townships would see the decision affect both future tax revenue and monthly utility costs.
stories.source_title: Calhoun County commissioners discuss data center moratorium and economic impacts - Yahoo

stories.title: Augusta Township nears vote on Oracle-scale data center rules
stories.dek: Augusta officials are set to decide on zoning and tax terms for a large data-center proposal that would pull power from the same DTE territory as Saline. The outcome determines how much new load hits ratepayers and whether the township collects meaningful revenue. A yes vote would add to the string of projects reshaping Washtenaw County's tax base and electricity demand.
stories.source_title: Weekly Data Center Watch: Saline Reverses Tax Break Limit as Augusta Vote Nears - The Sun Times News

stories.title: Dowagiac Residents Report Ongoing Noise From Operating Data Center, Explore Legal Options
stories.dek: Cooling systems at a local facility generate persistent humming that disrupts sleep, prompting complaints and mentions of potential class-action steps in the community.

stories.title: Saline Township flips, restoring Oracle's shot at big tax break
stories.dek: The board reversed its earlier limit on incentives for the proposed Oracle campus. Residents now face the prospect of forgoing years of property-tax revenue on a multibillion-dollar project that would draw heavy power and water. The change came after lobbying from the company and supporters who argue the facility would bring jobs.
stories.source_title: Saline Township reverses vote to limit Oracle's data center tax break - CBS News

stories.title: Delta County bans new data centers for a year
stories.dek: The county board voted to halt all data center proposals after residents raised concerns over noise, water use and strain on the rural grid. The one-year moratorium gives local officials time to write permanent zoning rules before any large projects can move forward. Power and water demands from even one hyperscale campus could affect rates and wells for years in this sparsely populated area.
stories.source_title: Delta County Board passes moratorium on data centers - DailyPress.net

stories.title: MLive map shows every active data center fight across Michigan
stories.dek: The new dashboard lets residents track proposals, hearings, and moratoria by township and county. It surfaces stakes like megawatts demanded and potential tax deals in real time. Users can see how projects near them compare to battles elsewhere in the state.
stories.source_title: MLive interactive dashboard tracks the data center battle raging across Michigan - MLive.com

stories.title: Wixom Keeps Data Center Pause to Shield Residents from Power Strain and Noise
stories.dek: City council rejected an early end to the moratorium on a three-building complex, citing risks to health and local resources that could raise resident costs.

stories.title: Williamston Township shelves data center zoning rules for now
stories.dek: Williamston Township postponed action on new data center regulations, leaving residents without clear limits on where massive facilities can locate. The delay keeps uncertainty high for nearby homes and farms over noise, power lines, and water use. Local officials gave no timeline for when rules might return.
stories.source_title: Williamstown Township tables proposed data center regulations - WILX

stories.title: Williamstown Township Delays Data Center Zoning Rules Until August
stories.dek: Planning commissioners tabled a proposed ordinance addressing setbacks, noise, water use and decommissioning after a public hearing where residents raised concerns about impacts on wildlife and resources.

national.title: Data centers advance rapidly as regulators scramble for rules
national.dek: Across the U.S., officials race to impose guardrails on power-hungry facilities while communities push back on incentives and impacts.
